title: Paying the bill
description: End every meal confidently — ask for the bill, split it, and pay by card
context: In Hong Kong you usually have to flag down a waiter to ask for the bill — it won't appear automatically. The phrase 埋單 (maai4 daan1) is what you need. Splitting the bill AA style (AA制) is very common among friends.
culturalFact: 八達通 (Octopus card) can be used to pay at many HK restaurants. Just look for the Octopus logo on the payment terminal. Card payments (VISA, Mastercard) are widely accepted in most sit-down restaurants.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: hai6 ji6 baak3 sei3 sap6 man1, m4 goi1.
lines.english: That's two hundred and forty dollars, thank you.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 可唔可以AA制？我哋四個人。
lines.jyutping: ho2 m4 ho2 ji5 AA zai3? ngo5 dei6 sei3 go3 jan4.
lines.english: Can we split it? There are four of us.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: dong1 jin4 ho2 ji5. mui5 jan4 luk6 sap6 man1.
lines.english: Of course. That's sixty dollars each.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: ho2 m4 ho2 ji5 caat3 kaat1?

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: ho2 ji5, VISA tung4 Mastercard dou1 dak1.
lines.english: Yes, we accept VISA and Mastercard.
